_comment: Plugin MCP wiring (vendor MCPs only). The write-capable ad platforms (Google Ads + Meta Ads) are NOT wired here -- they are SELF-HOSTED custom connectors with per-user OAuth: gads-mcp/ (Fly) for Google Ads, meta-ads-mcp/ (our VPS) for Meta Ads. The marketer clicks Connect and signs in with their own Google/Meta account, which is the true per-user auth the spend-gate relies on. See _custom_connectors_note below, CONNECTORS.md, GADS-SELF-HOST-RUNBOOK.md and META-SELF-HOST-RUNBOOK.md. Google Drive is NOT wired here - it lives in Claude desktop's native Connectors catalog (Customize -> Connectors -> Google Drive). NOTE on why this is not Composio: Claude desktop's Connect button only performs an OAuth handshake and has no field for an x-api-key / Bearer header (anthropics/claude-ai-mcp #112). Composio's hosted MCP endpoints authenticate with an x-api-key header, so they cannot be authorized from the Connect button - that mismatch caused the dynamic-client-registration failures. GA4 / Search Console / Tag Manager / Merchant Center do not yet have a verified OAuth MCP wired (see _pending_connectors below and CONNECTORS.md).

notion._comment: Optional. Briefs, playbooks in Notion. Vendor-native MCP.

supabase._comment: Already in use. Portfolio-site product/usage data. Vendor-native MCP.

vercel._comment: Already in use. Inspect deployments / scheduled data-pull jobs. Vendor-native MCP.

slack._comment: Optional. Share finished reports as messages. Vendor-native MCP.

_pending_connectors._comment: NOT wired yet - do NOT ship guessed URLs. Each needs a verified OAuth MCP endpoint (one a marketer can authorize from Claude desktop's Connect button) chosen and tested before team rollout. Candidates are listed for evaluation; once a URL is confirmed working in Claude desktop, move the entry up into mcpServers. See CONNECTORS.md section 'Pending connectors' for the decision log.

candidates: self-hosted google-analytics-mcp with Advanced-settings OAuth client ID

google-tag-manager.purpose: WRITE-CAPABLE: read containers/tags/triggers, create/edit, publish versions. Conversion-tracking changes are Tier 1 in the spend-gate.
google-tag-manager.note: No verified Connect-button OAuth MCP confirmed yet. Until one is wired, Tier-1 GTM tracking edits via /ad-actions are unavailable. Block team rollout of GTM writes until verified.

google-merchant-center.purpose: WRITE-CAPABLE: feed health, product attributes, supplemental feeds, promotions. Only relevant for brands running Shopping/PMax with a product feed.
google-merchant-center.note: Candidate fallback: the official Google Content API via a self-hosted MCP with Advanced-settings OAuth client ID.

_custom_connectors_note: Google Search Console and Google Ads are NOT plugin servers — Claude desktop's plugin/in-session OAuth flow is buggy (no localhost callback listener; 'a server with this URL already exists'). Marketers add them as CUSTOM CONNECTORS, which use Claude's hosted callback (claude.ai/api/mcp/auth_callback) and work. URLs: GSC = https://onlineminds-gsc-mcp.fly.dev/mcp ; Google Ads = https://onlineminds-gads-mcp.fly.dev/mcp . Both support DCR, so no Client ID/Secret to paste. See CONNECTORS.md and the setup guide. Meta Ads is ALSO a custom connector, now SELF-HOSTED on our VPS like Google Ads (one org-wide URL on the VPS, e.g. https://<meta-mcp-domain>/mcp) — Facebook login, per-user OAuth, writes gated by /ad-actions + READONLY_MODE. One URL serves every Business Manager: each marketer signs in with their own Facebook and only sees the accounts they manage. Built in meta-ads-mcp/ (mirrors gads-mcp); deploy per META-SELF-HOST-RUNBOOK.md. Replaces the former Meta-hosted mcp.meta.com per-BM URLs.
